title: Days Of Future Future

jokes.text: Please let it be a bear that did this.
jokes.setup: Homer has clearly done something destructive/embarrassing to himself
jokes.punchline: His hope that a bear was responsible instead of his own gluttony
jokes.explanation: Strong character-specific humor - only Homer would hope for bear attack over admitting gluttony. Economical and well-structured.

jokes.text: How much did I eat?
jokes.setup: Homer assessing damage from his eating
jokes.punchline: Treating eating like a destructive natural disaster
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer self-assessment after gluttony. Well-integrated but predictable.

jokes.text: I'm as healthy as a horse. / Horses only live 30 years.
jokes.setup: Homer claims to be healthy as a horse
jokes.punchline: Marge points out horses die young
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline structure. Marge's factual correction deflates Homer's confidence beautifully.

jokes.text: Heart exploding. Ooh. Pepperoni.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to be healthy by walking upstairs
jokes.punchline: Heart attack interrupted by food distraction
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Homer's fatal flaw literally killing him, then immediately distracted by food. Perfect economy.

jokes.text: All that's left are clever last words.
jokes.setup: Homer dying and needing final words
jokes.punchline: Meta-commentary on the expectation of clever death lines
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-humor about death scene conventions. Self-aware but not overly precious.

jokes.text: All he did was yell 'traveling' at the screen.
jokes.setup: Eulogy calling Homer a poorly informed sports fan
jokes.punchline: Specific example of his sports ignorance
jokes.explanation: Good specificity in the sports reference. Character-appropriate but not exceptional.

jokes.text: If, uh... you know, after a respectful period of grief and whatnot, uh... yous would like to have some coffee, please give a call.
jokes.setup: Moe offering condolences at Homer's funeral
jokes.punchline: Immediately hitting on the widow at the funeral
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe character moment - inappropriate timing with transparent motives. Predictable but well-executed.

jokes.text: I wanted to clone a sheep, but I needed to start with something simpler.
jokes.setup: Frink explaining why he cloned Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer is simpler than a sheep
jokes.explanation: Good insult disguised as scientific explanation. Clean structure and delivery.

jokes.text: Which he is, because his brain is fliveck.
jokes.setup: Frink explaining Homer's simplicity
jokes.punchline: Made-up scientific term for stupidity
jokes.explanation: Frink's pseudo-scientific vocabulary creates a quotable insult. Character-specific delivery.

jokes.text: Somebody kill me.
jokes.setup: Homer just died and was brought back to life
jokes.punchline: Immediately wants to die again upon hearing about Lisa's recital
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic timing - resurrection immediately followed by death wish. Economical and character-perfect.

jokes.text: Ah, hey, Midge. Uh... here we are again, ah? / Yeah... / And, oh, uh, here, uh, my new card.
jokes.setup: Another Homer death, another funeral
jokes.punchline: Moe treating it like a regular business opportunity
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier funeral scene but less impact. Shows Moe's persistent character trait.

jokes.text: I can die all I want. Frink's got Homers like the Tuileries has park benches.
jokes.setup: Homer realizing he can be reckless because he can be cloned
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and sophisticated comparison
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly sophisticated reference from Homer creates comedy through character contrast. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Huh? I wonder where I picked that up. / Oh. I planted a Western history chip, but all you retained were the places to sit down.
jokes.setup: Homer makes sophisticated reference, wonders about its origin
jokes.punchline: Educational chip only taught him about benches
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback explaining the previous joke while reinforcing Homer's selective stupidity. Great structure.

jokes.text: Talking Heads 'Once in a Lifetime' montage
jokes.setup: Multiple Homer deaths set to iconic song
jokes.punchline: Repetitive death/resurrection cycle with existential soundtrack
jokes.explanation: Clever musical choice for repetitive cycle. Visual montage humor with philosophical undertones.

jokes.text: Don't go far, Cremo.
jokes.setup: Crematorium robot arrives for another Homer death
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal implying this happens frequently
jokes.explanation: Good economy and implication of routine death. Solid but not exceptional.

jokes.text: You know what? No. I'm tired of getting my hopes up here. You and your immortal husband can go take a flying leap, huh?
jokes.setup: Moe's repeated failed attempts to date Marge
jokes.punchline: Finally giving up on widow pursuit
jokes.explanation: Good character development through repeated failure. Moe's frustration feels earned.

jokes.text: Stupid Moe. You just had to act like yourself, didn't you, you jackass?
jokes.setup: Moe blaming himself for ruining his chances
jokes.punchline: Self-awareness that being himself is the problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect self-aware character moment. Moe recognizing his own nature as self-sabotage.

jokes.text: Oh, you used up all your clones, you fat, fat, fat, reckless, fat pig!
jokes.setup: Frink explaining why Homer can't be revived
jokes.punchline: Increasingly emphatic repetition of 'fat'
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation humor. Repetition works but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: Bonk. Bonk. Bonk. Ooh. Corner. Ba-donk.
jokes.setup: Homer as a screen saver face bouncing around
jokes.punchline: Sound effects and corner excitement
jokes.explanation: Good visual concept with Homer as literal screensaver. Corner excitement is relatable.

jokes.text: I'm good.
jokes.setup: Homer reduced to a face on a screen
jokes.punchline: Casual acceptance of terrible situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer response - casual acceptance of absurd circumstances. Maximum economy.

jokes.text: If it wasn't for that squirrel that eats our bird food, we'd have nothing to talk about.
jokes.setup: Milhouse describing his marriage problems
jokes.punchline: Their only connection is complaining about a squirrel
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific detail about marital decay. Relatable and pathetically funny.

jokes.text: Nothing for 30 years. You're perfect for each other.
jokes.setup: Bart asks what he did wrong
jokes.punchline: Marge says he and Homer are perfect matches in laziness
jokes.explanation: Sharp maternal burn - efficient and character-appropriate

jokes.text: No. No. No. Don't pull me out before you click eject. That hurts like a mother...!
jokes.setup: Homer being ejected from computer
jokes.punchline: USB ejection protocol applied to person, cut off profanity
jokes.explanation: Clever application of computer terminology to human experience. Good physical comedy.

jokes.text: Why, you little...! I'll teach you to emulate my sloppiness!
jokes.setup: Homer angry at Bart in virtual reality
jokes.punchline: Computer terminology 'emulate' in traditional threat
jokes.explanation: Good tech-comedy wordplay. Character-appropriate adaptation of classic threat.

jokes.text: Living out of a car is not 'camping.'
jokes.setup: Bart claiming their previous relationship involved camping
jokes.punchline: Revealing it was actually homelessness
jokes.explanation: Sharp reality check revealing Bart's romanticizing of their failed relationship.

jokes.text: You're working with dinosaurs. / I miss my kids.
jokes.setup: Milhouse trying to cheer up Bart about his job
jokes.punchline: Bart's depression overrides the coolness of dinosaurs
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between childhood wonder and adult problems. Clean structure.

jokes.text: With social security a thing of the past, she can't afford to retire. I don't know how that happened in a senate with 99 Democrats. That one Republican is great at getting his way.
jokes.setup: Explaining why his elderly mother still works as stripper
jokes.punchline: Political satire about minority obstruction
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire with dark humor. Less character-specific but topically relevant.

jokes.text: Actually, Terrance, this is a synthetic substance. Devised for zombie vegans. Or 'zegans.'
jokes.setup: Zombie asking for brains
jokes.punchline: Vegan zombie portmanteau
jokes.explanation: Clever portmanteau addressing zombie dietary restrictions. Very quotable absurdist concept.

jokes.text: You, sir, am racist.
jokes.setup: Character making assumptions about zombies
jokes.punchline: Zombie's grammatically incorrect accusation
jokes.explanation: Grammar humor from zombie character. Economical and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Can I smell you later? / Why don't you smell me now?
jokes.setup: Bart attempting clever pickup line
jokes.punchline: Woman taking it literally
jokes.explanation: Standard pickup line humor. Well-structured but unoriginal concept.

jokes.text: You know, at my job, I satisfy ladies even bigger than you. / Um, I feed dinosaurs.
jokes.setup: Bart making what sounds like sexual boast
jokes.punchline: Innocent explanation about feeding dinosaurs
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection joke. Perfectly timed revelation that recontextualizes the setup.

jokes.text: You have, like, a goat beard stuck to your shoe.
jokes.setup: Bart being ejected for being depressing
jokes.punchline: Random specific detail from his dinosaur job
jokes.explanation: Good specific detail connecting to his job. Adds texture to the world-building.

jokes.text: Man, that's some targeted advertising.
jokes.setup: Bart seeing ad for divorce therapy after his divorce talk
jokes.punchline: Commentary on invasive digital advertising
jokes.explanation: Relatable modern observation about digital privacy. Timely but not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: It'll be over before I finish this... ...sentence.
jokes.setup: Doctor promising quick procedure
jokes.punchline: Self-referential timing joke with dramatic pause
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about comedic timing. Well-executed but familiar concept.

jokes.text: Window sushi fake? / Window sushi fake.
jokes.setup: Zombie learning display sushi isn't real
jokes.punchline: Disappointed zombie realization
jokes.explanation: Good zombie character moment with relatable restaurant experience. Economical dialogue.

jokes.text: 'Good' is not a concept in my culture!
jokes.setup: Sexual performance question
jokes.punchline: Existential response about cultural concepts
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly philosophical response to sexual question. Absurd and quotable.

jokes.text: There's Beppo, Boppo, Emmett Kelly the Ninth, Frenchy, Insane Clown Polly and Commodore Tee-hee.
jokes.setup: Bart discovering he went home with multiple people
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous clown names
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with creative clown names. Solid absurdist humor.

jokes.text: There, back up.
jokes.setup: Officer requesting backup
jokes.punchline: Wiggum literally backing up
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum literal interpretation. Maximum economy and character integration.

jokes.text: No breathing, no asthma.
jokes.setup: Milhouse as zombie fighting crime
jokes.punchline: Death solved his asthma problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark humor about death's benefits. Maximum economy with character integration.

jokes.text: Brevity soul wit.
jokes.setup: Lisa commenting on his dropping articles
jokes.punchline: Milhouse demonstrating by abbreviating Shakespeare quote
jokes.explanation: Brilliant demonstration of the concept through execution. Self-referential and perfectly economical.

jokes.text: It's only natural to miss a man you buried 127 times.
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining why Marge misses Homer
jokes.punchline: Specific high number of burials
jokes.explanation: Good callback to repeated deaths with specific number. Absurd but logical.

jokes.text: Hey, where's my junk?
jokes.setup: Homer getting new robot body
jokes.punchline: Immediate concern about genitals
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Homer priority. Economical and character-specific but predictable.

jokes.text: Man, when is that voice gonna change?
jokes.setup: Waiter with high voice serving desserts
jokes.punchline: Bart assumes it's puberty rather than adult voice
jokes.explanation: Mild joke about adult with high voice

jokes.text: Threat perceived. Terminate! Terminate!
jokes.setup: Homer hugging his grandchildren
jokes.punchline: Robot body interprets affection as threat
jokes.explanation: Good robot malfunction humor. Dark comedy of affection being misinterpreted as violence.

jokes.text: Like a classic rock band whose hold-out member finally needs money.
jokes.setup: Family getting back together
jokes.punchline: Cynical but accurate music industry comparison
jokes.explanation: Sharp cultural observation with cynical but accurate insight. Very quotable.

jokes.text: I am not stopping again. / But I had a large soda! / I said get the small! / It was only 50 cents more!
jokes.setup: Evil self-driving car refusing to stop
jokes.punchline: Mundane road trip argument between human and AI
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist concept - evil AI still having normal road trip problems. Relatable escalation.

jokes.text: Until I was eight, I thought he was a magical gorilla.
jokes.setup: Marge explaining why she stayed with Homer
jokes.punchline: Childhood misunderstanding of Homer's nature
jokes.explanation: Exceptional character insight disguised as absurd childhood memory. Perfectly captures both characters in one line.

jokes.text: Or my decision to support Duffman in his old age. / Duffman can still swing and party. Oh, yeah.
jokes.setup: Marge listing her heart-based decisions
jokes.punchline: Aging Duffman still maintaining his persona
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency joke. Duffman maintaining persona despite age is endearing.

jokes.text: I can't tell if that was love, suicide or a... a really boring video game.
jokes.setup: Marge joining Homer in digital form
jokes.punchline: Three increasingly mundane interpretations
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from profound to mundane. Lisa's analytical nature fits perfectly.

jokes.text: Everything that you experienced between when I flipped the switch and now was just a neural implant in your temporal lobe.
jokes.setup: Apparent resolution of Bart's story
jokes.punchline: Reveal that most of episode was false memory
jokes.explanation: Classic 'it was all a dream' twist. Clever structurally but somewhat cheap narratively.

jokes.text: We treat a lot of people for obsessions. It's a whole new day for Nothing Stu!
jokes.setup: Doctor explaining their practice
jokes.punchline: Accidental revelation about treating 'Disco Stu'
jokes.explanation: Good callback to classic character. Wordplay works with character's typical naming pattern.

jokes.text: Dead flower from dead husband.
jokes.setup: Zombie Milhouse giving Lisa a flower
jokes.punchline: Romantic gesture with morbid accuracy
jokes.explanation: Perfect zombie romantic gesture. Maximum economy with dark humor and character consistency.

tmdb_overview: Set in the future, Homer has a new clone for every time he dies. Lisa is married to a zombie version of Milhouse, while Bart deals with custody issues with his ex-wife, Jenda. Each defines what love means to them.
